text: Perfect. I'm so glad we were able to resolve that for you.
Oh, sorry, I didn't realize we were filming.
I was just finishing a customer call.
When customers are out in the field or on the road,
they can't drop everything to shoot you a text.
So instead, they call.
But trying to manage phone support used to mean stitching together like five different tools.
You have a call platform, contact center, your existing support platform,
but now Pylon has native phone support.
You can answer customer calls, transfer them, manage voicemails and recordings, all within Pylon.
So when a customer calls in, it automatically creates a support ticket
and pulls up full account context.
Whoever picks up already knows who they're talking to.
And when you need to call a customer yourself,
you can do it directly from a support issue.
No dialing out, no copy-pacing numbers, no switching apps.
The call automatically gets logs and tied to the right account.
Every customer phone call now lives alongside your SMS conversations
and support interactions from every single other channel.
It's one complete view of your customer relationships all in Pylon.
Oh, I gotta go. Customer on the line.
Hey, Jonah, how can I help?
